=== PLAN ===
Build a browser-based Yew/Rust/WASM APL environment for COR24. Single-panel REPL with printer-style emulation, floating hardware panel (S2 switch, D2 LED, UART TX/RX), demo dropdown, file upload, and reset. Phase 1: scaffold Yew project with Trunk, wire cor24-emulator path dep. Phase 2: REPL panel (scrolling output, 6-space indent prompt, keyboard capture) + control bar. Phase 3: emulator integration (EmulatorBridge, UART I/O bridge, batch execution, pre-assemble APL interpreter via build.rs). Phase 4: hardware panel + demo registry + file upload. Phase 5: polish, responsive design, GitHub Pages deployment. Phase 6: APL glyph prettification display modes.

=== STEPS ===
  [x] 001-scaffold-yew-project [production]: Scaffold the Yew/Rust/WASM project for web-sw-cor24-apl. Follow the exact pat...
  [x] 002-repl-panel [legacy]: Create the REPL panel component. Follow the patterns from web-sw-cor24-pcode'...
  [x] 003-003-emulator-integration [production]: Wire the COR24 emulator into the REPL panel so that APL input is evaluated by... <-- YOU ARE HERE

=== CURRENT STEP: 003-003-emulator-integration [production] ===
Status: completed
Description: Wire the COR24 emulator into the REPL panel so that APL input is evaluated by...
Task type: integration

=== PROMPT ===
Wire the COR24 emulator into the REPL panel so that APL input is evaluated by the real interpreter running on the emulator.

1. Update build.rs to pre-assemble the APL interpreter (../sw-cor24-apl/build/apl.s) at build time using cor24_emulator::Assembler. Write the binary to OUT_DIR/apl.bin. No label extraction needed (APL binary is a self-contained program).

2. Create src/config.rs with the pre-assembled APL binary (include_bytes) — same pattern as web-sw-cor24-pcode's config.rs but simpler (just the binary, no labels).

3. Modify src/repl.rs to integrate EmulatorCore:
   - Add EmulatorCore, StopReason, VecDeque fields to ReplPanel struct
   - Add Msg::Init to load the APL binary and start running
   - Add Msg::Tick for batch execution (50k instructions per 25ms tick)
   - On Enter: push input bytes + newline into uart_rx_queue instead of echoing
   - Add feed_uart_bytes() to drain rx queue checking UART status register 0xFF0101 bit 0
   - Add collect_uart() to read emulator UART output and append to output buffer
   - The APL interpreter uses getchar/putchar mapped to UART — output appears via UART TX
   - Auto-start running on Init (not paused like the debugger)
   - Keep the blinking cursor

4. Update src/lib.rs to add mod config.

5. Verify trunk build compiles successfully.

=== PAST SUCCESSES (integration) ===
  [2026-03-30T19:09:13] action=read pcode debugger pattern, adapted EmulatorCore integration, build.rs assembler, config module, UART bridge, result=success, reward=+1

=== WHEN DONE ===
Run: agentrail begin    (if not yet started)
Run: agentrail complete --summary "what you did" --next-slug <slug> --next-prompt "next instructions"
  Or: agentrail complete --summary "what you did" --done   (if this is the last step)

You are bootstrapping a new web UI project: web-sw-cor24-apl -- a browser-based
APL environment (REPL/editor) running on the COR24 emulator via WASM.

Follow these steps in order.

## Step 1: Learn agentrail

Run:
```
agentrail --help
```
Understand the commands available (init, status, next, begin, complete, plan,
history, add, setup, abort).

## Step 2: Study an existing web project with agentrail

Look at sibling projects under ~/github/sw-vibe-coding/ and ~/github/sw-embed/
that have `.agentrail/` directories. Good references:

- ~/github/sw-embed/web-sw-cor24-pcode/ (closest match -- COR24 web debugger)
- ~/github/sw-vibe-coding/web-tml24c/ (Lisp REPL on COR24)
- ~/github/sw-vibe-coding/web-tc24r/ (C compiler browser UI)

From one of these (preferably web-sw-cor24-pcode), study:
- CLAUDE.md -- the AgentRail Session Protocol and project structure
- .agentrail/saga.toml -- saga config
- .agentrail/plan.md -- the phased plan
- .agentrail/steps/*/step.toml -- step configs (number, slug, description, role, task_type)
- Cargo.toml -- dependencies (Yew 0.21, wasm-bindgen, web-sys, gloo, etc.)
- index.html -- Trunk directives, Catppuccin Mocha theme, layout
- scripts/ -- serve.sh, build-pages.sh patterns
- src/ -- Yew Component architecture (Component trait, Msg enum, html! macro)
- pages/ -- GitHub Pages output directory with .nojekyll
- build.rs -- build metadata and pre-processing patterns

Then create a CLAUDE.md for THIS project (~/github/sw-embed/web-sw-cor24-apl/)
that follows the same pattern. It must include:
- The full AgentRail Session Protocol (6 steps: next, begin, work, commit, complete, stop)
- Project description (browser-based APL environment on COR24)
- Related projects (sw-cor24-apl, sw-cor24-emulator, sw-cor24-assembler, etc.)
- Build commands (trunk via scripts, clippy, fmt)
- Available task types (rust-project-init, yew-component, wasm-build, etc.)
- Edition 2024, never suppress warnings

## Step 3: Understand the web UI tech stack

From studying the reference projects, note the established patterns:
- Rust edition 2024
- Yew 0.21 with CSR (client-side rendering only, no server backend)
- Trunk as the build tool (not webpack/Vite)
- wasm-bindgen + web-sys for browser APIs
- Path dependencies to sibling compiler/emulator repos (e.g., ../sw-cor24-apl, ../sw-cor24-emulator)
- Catppuccin Mocha dark theme
- pages/ directory committed to git, deployed via GitHub Pages
- scripts/serve.sh for dev, scripts/build-pages.sh for release
- build.rs for compile-time pre-processing and metadata

The live demo will be served from the pages/ directory via GitHub Pages at a
URL like: https://softwarewrighter.github.io/web-sw-cor24-apl/

## Step 4: Read the base project research

Read ~/github/sw-embed/sw-cor24-apl/docs/research.txt thoroughly. This is the
comprehensive design document for the APL implementation. Key aspects:
- ASCII surface syntax: lowercase reserved words (iota, rho, take, drop, etc.)
- Uppercase user identifiers (A, FOO, MATRIX1)
- Context-sensitive display: monadic rho = "shape-of", dyadic A rho B = "reshape"
- Editor prettification: display rho as the APL glyph but store canonical ASCII
- Integer-only APL, rank <= 2, small arrays
- Historical APL implementations for context

Also read ~/github/sw-embed/sw-cor24-apl/Cargo.toml and any other docs/ files
to understand the current state of the base APL project.

## Step 5: Ask questions

Before proceeding to documentation, ask me any questions you have about:
- Scope of the web UI (full IDE? minimal REPL? visual debugger?)
- Which APL features to prioritize in the UI
- Desired UI layout (single panel REPL? multi-panel editor + output?)
- Whether the APL interpreter is ready to embed or needs stubbing
- Integration approach with cor24-emulator (direct embed vs. compile-and-run)
- Any preferences for the architecture

## Step 6: Create documentation

Based on everything you've learned, create these four documents in ./docs/:

### docs/architecture.md
High-level system architecture:
- Component diagram (Yew components, APL interpreter, COR24 emulator)
- Data flow (user input -> parse -> compile -> emulate -> display)
- WASM build pipeline (Trunk -> wasm-bindgen -> pages/)
- Path dependency structure to sibling repos
- How the APL prettification layer works in the browser

### docs/prd.md
Product requirements document:
- Target user and use case
- Core features (REPL, editor, output display, APL glyph rendering)
- Non-functional requirements (performance, browser compatibility, offline)
- What's in scope vs. out of scope for v1
- Success criteria

### docs/plan.md
Implementation plan (phased, matching agentrail saga step pattern):
- Phase 1: Scaffold Yew project, wire dependencies, basic Trunk build
- Phase 2: REPL input/output panel
- Phase 3: APL expression evaluation (wire interpreter)
- Phase 4+: Additional features based on PRD
- Each phase should map to 1-2 agentrail saga steps

### docs/design.md
Detailed UI/UX design:
- Layout wireframe description (panels, toolbar, status bar)
- Catppuccin Mocha theme integration
- APL glyph rendering approach (ASCII input -> Unicode display)
- Keyboard input handling for APL operators
- Responsive design considerations
- Component hierarchy (Yew Component tree)

content.content:
# web-sw-cor24-apl -- Implementation Plan

## Overview

Phased plan for building the browser-based APL environment. Each phase
maps to 1-2 agentrail saga steps. Follows the same patterns established
in web-sw-cor24-pcode.

## Phase 1: Scaffold Yew Project (1 step)

**Step: rust-project-init**

- Configure Cargo.toml: edition 2024, Yew 0.21 (csr), wasm-bindgen,
  web-sys, gloo, console_error_panic_hook, cor24-emulator path dep
- Set crate-type = ["cdylib", "rlib"]
- Create index.html with Catppuccin Mocha theme, Trunk directives,
  header ("APL" / "COR24 Environment"), footer with build metadata,
  GitHub corner link to sw-embed/web-sw-cor24-apl
- Create src/lib.rs with App function_component
- Create src/main.rs with Yew renderer + panic hook
- Create scripts/serve.sh (port 9957)
- Create scripts/build-pages.sh (--public-url /web-sw-cor24-apl/)
- Create build.rs with git SHA, hostname, timestamp metadata
- Verify `trunk build` compiles and serves a hello-world page

## Phase 2: REPL Panel (1-2 steps)

**Step: yew-component (REPL shell)**

- Create ReplPanel component with:
  - Scrolling output area (div with overflow-y: auto)
  - Input line at bottom with 6-space indent
  - Keyboard capture (onkeydown) with backspace support
  - Output buffer (Vec<String>) rendered as lines
  - Printer-style behavior: new output appends, panel auto-scrolls
- Style with Catppuccin Mocha colors (base background, text foreground,
  blue for prompt, green for output)
- Initially: echo input back as output (no emulator yet)

**Step: yew-component (control bar)**

- Demo dropdown (empty options initially, wired in Phase 4)
- File upload button (wired in Phase 4)
- Reset button (clears output buffer initially)

## Phase 3: Emulator Integration (1-2 steps)

**Step: integration (emulator bridge)**

- Create EmulatorBridge: instantiate EmulatorCore, load APL interpreter
  binary into memory
- Build.rs: pre-assemble APL interpreter (from sw-cor24-apl C source
  via tc24r -> cor24-assembler) at build time, embed as static bytes
- UART I/O bridge: input ring buffer (keystrokes -> UART 0xFF0100),
  output ring buffer (UART output -> display)
- Batch execution loop (gloo::timers::Timeout, ~50K instructions/tick)
- Wire ReplPanel input to UART TX, UART RX to ReplPanel output

**Step: integration (verify APL REPL)**

- Verify: type expression, see result
- Test: `iota 10`, `2 3 rho iota 6`, `+/ iota 100`
- Handle emulator startup sequence (APL interpreter init, prompt)

## Phase 4: Hardware Panel + Demos (1-2 steps)

**Step: yew-component (hardware panel)**

- Floating panel with:
  - S2 switch (initially off, click to toggle)
  - D2 LED (initially off, reflects emulator LED register 0xFF0000)
  - UART TX field (shows last sent byte, future use)
  - UART RX field (shows last received byte, future use)
- CSS: floating/positioned panel, semi-transparent background,
  draggable (stretch goal)

**Step: feature (demo registry + file upload)**

- Demo registry: pre-assembled .apl programs embedded via build.rs
- Demo dropdown populated with demo names

- Create EmulatorBridge: instantiate EmulatorCore, load APL interpreter
  binary into memory
- Build.rs: pre-assemble APL interpreter (from sw-cor24-apl C source
  via tc24r -> cor24-assembler) at build time, embed as static bytes
- UART I/O bridge: input ring buffer (keystrokes -> UART 0xFF0100),
  output ring buffer (UART output -> display)
- Batch execution loop (gloo::timers::Timeout, ~50K instructions/tick)
- Wire ReplPanel input to UART TX, UART RX to ReplPanel output

**Step: integration (verify APL REPL)**

- Verify: type expression, see result
- Test: `iota 10`, `2 3 rho iota 6`, `+/ iota 100`
- Handle emulator startup sequence (APL interpreter init, prompt)

## Phase 4: Hardware Panel + Demos (1-2 steps)

**Step: yew-component (hardware panel)**

- Floating panel with:
  - S2 switch (initially off, click to toggle)
  - D2 LED (initially off, reflects emulator LED register 0xFF0000)
  - UART TX field (shows last sent byte, future use)
  - UART RX field (shows last received byte, future use)
- CSS: floating/positioned panel, semi-transparent background,
  draggable (stretch goal)

**Step: feature (demo registry + file upload)**

- Demo registry: pre-assembled .apl programs embedded via build.rs
- Demo dropdown populated with demo names

# web-sw-cor24-apl -- UI/UX Design

## Layout Overview

Single-page application with a full-height REPL panel as the primary
interface. A floating hardware panel overlays the REPL. A control bar
sits between the header and the REPL panel.

```
+----------------------------------------------------------+
| Header: "APL" | "COR24 Environment"        [GitHub corner]|
+----------------------------------------------------------+
| Control Bar: [Demo v] [Upload] [Reset]                    |
+----------------------------------------------------------+
|                                                           |
|  REPL Panel (scrolling output area)                       |
|                                                           |
|        A <- 2 3 rho iota 6                                |
|  0 1 2                                                    |
|  3 4 5                                                    |
|        +/ iota 100                                        |
|  4950                                                     |
|        _  <-- cursor here, 6-space indent                 |
|                                                           |
|  +------------------+                                     |
|  | Hardware Panel   |                                     |
|  | S2: [off]        |                                     |
|  | D2: (o)          |                                     |
|  | TX: --  RX: --   |                                     |
|  +------------------+                                     |
|                                                           |
+----------------------------------------------------------+
| Footer: MIT | (c) 2026 MAW | COR24-TB | sha | host | ts |
+----------------------------------------------------------+
```

## REPL Panel Behavior

The REPL emulates a **printer terminal** (not a screen editor):

- Output scrolls upward continuously
- Input only happens at the bottom line
- No cursor movement up/down or editing previous lines
- Backspace erases the last typed character on the current line
- Enter submits the current line to the interpreter
- After submission, output appears below, then a new prompt

### Prompt

- 6 spaces of indentation (traditional APL convention)
- No prompt character -- just the indent
- Cursor blinks after the indent (or after typed characters)

### Input Handling

- Printable ASCII characters append to current input line
- Backspace: delete last character (stop at indent boundary)
- Enter: submit line to UART TX bridge
- No tab, no arrow keys (initially)
- Focus: REPL panel captures keyboard when page is focused

### Output Display

- Interpreter output appears line by line as received from UART RX
- Each output line left-aligned (interpreter handles formatting)
- Matrices: one row per line, right-justified columns (done by
  the APL interpreter, not the browser)
- Errors: displayed as-is from interpreter (e.g., "SYNTAX ERROR")

### Scrolling

- Auto-scroll to bottom on new output or input
- User can scroll up to review history
- Scroll lock: if user has scrolled up, don't auto-scroll
  (resume auto-scroll when user scrolls to bottom)

## Hardware Panel

Floating panel in the bottom-right corner of the REPL area.

### S2 Switch

- Visual toggle switch (on/off)
- Initially off
- Maps to COR24 emulator switch input
- Click to toggle

### D2 LED

- Circular indicator (lit = green, off = dark)
- Initially off
- Reflects emulator LED register (0xFF0000)
- Updated each execution tick

### UART TX/RX Fields

- Small text fields showing last byte sent/received
- For future diagnostic use
- Initially display "--" or "00"

### Panel Styling

- Semi-transparent dark background (--mantle with opacity)
- Compact layout, small font
- Positioned bottom-right, above footer
- Does not block REPL input line
- Future: draggable

## Control Bar

Horizontal bar below the header.

### Demo Dropdown

- HTML select element
- First option: "(select demo)" disabled placeholder
- Options populated from demo registry (build-time embedded)
- Selecting a demo: feeds program to interpreter via UART
- After execution, interpreter returns to REPL prompt

### File Upload Button

- Hidden file input + styled button ("Upload .apl")
- Accepts .apl and .txt files
- On upload: read file contents, feed to interpreter via UART
- Large files: feed line by line with pacing (avoid UART overflow)

### Reset Button

- Styled button ("Reset")
- Resets emulator (reload APL interpreter binary)
- Clears REPL output buffer
- Returns to fresh workspace with 6-space indent prompt

## Catppuccin Mocha Theme

All colors from the Catppuccin Mocha palette:

| Element            | Color variable | Hex       |
|--------------------|---------------|-----------|
| Page background    | --base        | #1e1e2e   |
| Panel background   | --mantle      | #181825   |
| Surface borders    | --surface1    | #45475a   |
| Primary text       | --text        | #cdd6f4   |
| Header title       | --blue        | #89b4fa   |
| Prompt indent      | --text        | #cdd6f4   |
| User input text    | --text        | #cdd6f4   |
| Output text        | --green       | #a6e3a1   |
| Error text         | --red         | #f38ba8   |
| LED on             | --green       | #a6e3a1   |
| LED off            | --surface0    | #313244   |
| Switch on          | --blue        | #89b4fa   |
| Switch off         | --surface1    | #45475a   |
| Button             | --surface0    | #313244   |
| Button hover       | --surface1    | #45475a   |
| Footer text        | --overlay0    | #6c7086   |
| Links              | --blue        | #89b4fa   |

Font: JetBrains Mono / Fira Code / Cascadia Code / monospace fallback.
Font size: 15px base, 0.85em footer.

## Component Hierarchy (Yew)

```
App
  +-- Header (static HTML in App)
  +-- ControlBar
  |     +-- DemoDropdown (select element)
  |     +-- FileUpload (input[type=file] + button)
  |     +-- ResetButton
  +-- ReplPanel
  |     +-- OutputArea (scrolling div)
  |     +-- InputLine (captures keyboard)
  +-- HardwarePanel (floating)
  |     +-- S2Switch
  |     +-- D2Led
  |     +-- UartDisplay
  +-- Footer (static HTML in App)
```

State lives primarily in the top-level App or a shared context:
- `output_lines: Vec<String>` -- REPL output history
- `input_buffer: String` -- current input line
- `emulator: EmulatorCore` -- the running emulator instance
- `demos: Vec<Demo>` -- embedded demo programs

## APL Glyph Rendering (Future)

Three display modes, toggled by a control:

### Representation Mode (v1 default)
Display exactly what the interpreter outputs: `rho`, `iota`, etc.
No transformation.

### Shorthand Mode (future)
Post-process output to replace ASCII tokens with Unicode glyphs:
- `rho` -> `⍴`
- `iota` -> `⍳`
- `take` -> `↑`
- `drop` -> `↓`
- `rev` -> `⌽`
- `cat` -> `,`

Applied as a display filter on UART RX output before rendering.

### Literate Mode (future)
Context-sensitive replacement using arity detection:
- monadic `rho X` -> `shape-of X`
- dyadic `A rho B` -> `A reshape B`

Requires lightweight parsing of the display output. More complex,
deferred to Phase 6.

## Responsive Considerations

- REPL panel fills available height (flex: 1)
- Hardware panel repositions or collapses on narrow viewports
- Minimum viable width: ~400px (phone landscape)
- No horizontal scroll in REPL (long lines wrap or truncate)
- Control bar wraps on narrow screens

## Summary Report

Based on my exploration of `/Users/mike/github/sw-embed/web-sw-cor24-apl/`, here are my findings:

### Current State of Implementation

This is a **Rust/Yew WASM-based APL interpreter environment** running the COR24 emulator in the browser. Currently, only the **REPL panel** has been implemented. The control bar, demo dropdown, file upload, and reset button features are **planned but not yet implemented**.

---

## File Locations and Contents

### 1. **REPL Panel Implementation** (Currently the only working component)

**File**: `/Users/mike/github/sw-embed/web-sw-cor24-apl/src/repl.rs` (232 lines)

This is the main interactive component. Key implementation details:

- **Component Structure**: `ReplPanel` struct with message enum `Msg`
- **State Management**:
  - `emulator: EmulatorCore` - the COR24 CPU emulator
  - `output: Vec<String>` - REPL output history
  - `input: String` - current input line being typed
  - `cursor_visible: bool` - blinking cursor state
  - `running/halted` - execution state flags
  - `uart_rx_queue: VecDeque<u8>` - queued input bytes to send to emulator
  - `partial_line: String` - buffered incomplete output lines

- **Message Types**:
  - `KeyDown(KeyboardEvent)` - keyboard input handling
  - `Blink` - cursor blink animation
  - `Init` - initialization
  - `Tick` - execution batch loop

- **Key Methods**:
  - `load_apl_binary()` - loads pre-assembled APL interpreter from config
  - `feed_uart_bytes()` - sends queued input to emulator UART
  - `collect_uart()` - reads emulator UART output
  - `schedule_blink()` / `schedule_tick()` - timer scheduling

- **Input Handling**:
  - Printable ASCII characters added to input
  - Backspace erases last character
  - Enter submits line to UART queue
  - PROMPT is hardcoded as 6 spaces: `"      "`

- **Rendering**:
  - Scrolling output area with monospace font
  - Blue prompt, white input text, green output text
  - Blinking cursor after input

**Execution Flow**:
1. `Msg::Init` loads the APL binary via `config::APL_BINARY`
2. Every 25ms, `Msg::Tick` runs up to 50,000 CPU instructions
3. Input bytes are fed to emulator UART
4. UART output is collected and rendered
5. When emulator halts, execution stops

---

### 2. **APL Binary Configuration**

**File**: `/Users/mike/github/sw-embed/web-sw-cor24-apl/src/config.rs` (6 lines)

```rust
pub const APL_BINARY: &[u8] = include_bytes!(concat!(env!("OUT_DIR"), "/apl.bin"));
```

The APL interpreter binary is **embedded as a compile-time constant**. It comes from:
- **Source**: `../sw-cor24-apl/build/apl.s` (COR24 assembly)
- **Built by**: `build.rs` using the `Assembler` from `cor24_emulator`
- **Output**: Written to `target/OUT_DIR/apl.bin` and included at compile time

---

### 3. **Build Process**

**File**: `/Users/mike/github/sw-embed/web-sw-cor24-apl/build.rs` (43 lines)

- Reads and assembles `../sw-cor24-apl/build/apl.s` using `cor24_emulator::Assembler`
- Outputs binary to `OUT_DIR/apl.bin`
- Captures build metadata:
  - Git short SHA
  - Build hostname
  - ISO timestamp
- Exports via `cargo:rustc-env`

---

### 4. **UI Structure**

**File**: `/Users/mike/github/sw-embed/web-sw-cor24-apl/src/lib.rs` (56 lines)

The `App` component structure:
```rust
#[function_component(App)]
pub fn app() -> Html {
    // GitHub corner link
    // Header: "APL" | "COR24 Environment"
    // Main content: <ReplPanel />
    // Footer: license, copyright, links, build metadata
}
```

---

### 5. **Styling**

**Files**: 
- `/Users/mike/github/sw-embed/web-sw-cor24-apl/index.html` (120 lines) - Catppuccin Mocha theme CSS
- `/Users/mike/github/sw-embed/web-sw-cor24-apl/src/app.css` (60 lines) - REPL panel styles

**Color Scheme**: Catppuccin Mocha palette with CSS variables
- Background: `#1e1e2e`
- Prompt: Blue `#89b4fa`
- Output: Green `#a6e3a1`
- Text: `#cdd6f4`

---

## Missing Features (Planned but Not Implemented)

Per the **Design Document** (`/Users/mike/github/sw-embed/web-sw-cor24-apl/docs/design.md`):

### 1. **Control Bar Component**
- Should appear between header and REPL panel
- Contains: Demo Dropdown, File Upload button, Reset button
- **Not yet implemented**

### 2. **Demo Dropdown**
- Intended to load pre-built APL programs
- Demos would be embedded at build time
- On selection, feeds program to interpreter via UART
- **No demo registry or dropdown component exists**

### 3. **File Upload**
- Hidden file input + styled button
- Accepts `.apl` and `.txt` files
- Feeds file contents line-by-line to interpreter via UART with pacing
- **Not implemented**

### 4. **Reset Button**
- Should reset emulator (reload APL binary)
- Clear REPL output buffer
- Return to fresh workspace
- **Not implemented** (though the infrastructure exists in `repl.rs` via `load_apl_binary()`)

### 5. **Hardware Panel** (Floating)
- S2 Switch (toggleable on/off)
- D2 LED (visual indicator)
- UART TX/RX diagnostic fields
- Should float in bottom-right corner
- **Not implemented**

---

## Key Technical Insights

1. **Demo Programs**: Would need to be:
   - Assembled from `.s` (assembly) or APL source files at build time
   - Embedded as constants (like `APL_BINARY`)
   - Registered in a demo registry struct
   - Fed to emulator via UART

2. **File Upload**: Would leverage the existing UART bridge:
   - Read file bytes via `web_sys::File`
   - Queue bytes in `uart_rx_queue`
   - Feed to emulator via `feed_uart_bytes()`

3. **Reset Button**: Infrastructure mostly exists:
   - `load_apl_binary()` already resets state
   - Would need to be called from a button click message

4. **Architecture**: All state lives in `ReplPanel` component - future components (`ControlBar`, `HardwarePanel`, etc.) would need to:
   - Be sibling components in `App`
   - Share state via a context/provider pattern or parent-level state management
   - Or be refactored into `App` directly

---

# /mw-cp — Mike Wright's Checkpoint Process

You are executing a disciplined checkpoint commit. Follow every phase in order.
Do NOT skip any phase, even if it seems clean. Report status after each phase.

---

## Phase 1 — Pre-Commit Checks

Run all of the following that apply to this project. Stop and report failures before proceeding.

1. **Format**: `cargo fmt --check` (or `rustfmt --check` on specific files)
2. **Lint**: `cargo clippy -- -D warnings`
3. **Build**: `cargo build` (debug is fine; surface any compile errors)
4. **Tests**: `cargo test` — all tests must pass
5. **Unused deps** (if `cargo-machete` present): `cargo machete`
6. **Project-local checks**: run `just check` or `just pre-commit` if a justfile exists

If any check fails, STOP. Report what failed and ask the user how to proceed.

---

## Phase 2 — Documentation Updates

Review recent changes and update the following as appropriate:

1. **CHANGELOG.md / CHANGES.md** — add an entry under `[Unreleased]` or today's date
2. **STATUS.md** — update current state, what changed, what's in progress
3. **LEARNINGS.md** — capture anything notable: decisions made, gotchas, tradeoffs
4. **Inline docs** — ensure any new public API items have doc comments (`///`)
5. **README.md** — update if any user-facing behavior, config, or setup changed

If none of these files exist, note that and skip gracefully.

---

## Phase 3 — Staged Diff Review

Run `git diff --staged` (or `git diff HEAD` if nothing staged yet).

Summarize what is changing:
- Files modified, added, deleted
- High-level functional description of the change

Then stage all changes: `git add -A`

---

## Phase 4 — Commit

Compose a detailed commit message following Conventional Commits style:
```
<type>(<scope>): <short imperative summary>

<body — explain WHAT changed and WHY, not HOW>
<mention any tradeoffs, alternatives considered, or follow-ups needed>

Refs: <issue/ticket numbers if applicable>
Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
```

Types: `feat`, `fix`, `refactor`, `docs`, `test`, `chore`, `perf`, `build`

If  was provided, incorporate it as context for the message.

Execute: `git commit -m "<message>"`

---

## Phase 5 — Push

1. Check current branch: `git branch --show-current`
2. Check remote: `git remote -v`
3. Push: `git push origin <branch>`

If the push is rejected (non-fast-forward), STOP and report — do NOT force push without explicit user confirmation.

---

## Phase 6 — Checkpoint Summary

Report a brief summary:
- ✅ Checks passed
- 📝 Docs updated (list files)
- 🔖 Commit hash and message
- 🚀 Pushed to remote/branch

Flag any items that were skipped or need follow-up.
```

---

## Usage
```
/mw-cp
/mw-cp adds OAuth token refresh with retry logic

git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(ui): add control bar, hardware panel, and demo registry

Introduce three new components for the APL environment:

- ControlBar: Reset button (restarts emulator), demo dropdown with
  6 pre-built APL programs in ASCII syntax, and file upload for
  .apl/.txt files via FileReader API
- HardwarePanel: floating bottom-right overlay showing S2 switch
  toggle, D2 LED indicator (reads 0xFF0000), and UART TX/RX byte
  display — matches COR24 hardware emulation pattern
- Demo registry: Scalars, Variables, Vectors, Iota & Reduce,
  Reshape, Matrices — all in COR24 ASCII syntax (rho/iota/take/drop)

Refactored App from function_component to full Component to manage
state flow between components. ReplPanel now accepts Properties for
reset/feed commands (sequence-counter pattern) and reports hardware
state upward via callback. S2 switch state written to 0xFF0200.

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
